description_long: This is a small, preserved bird specimen with a streamlined body and a short, conical beak. Its plumage is primarily muted brown and gray, with a slightly lighter underside and subtle gradations along the wings and tail. The wings are folded tightly against the body, and the tail is moderately long and appears straight with a slightly rounded tip. The legs are thin and dark, partially visible beneath the body. The overall appearance suggests a passerine bird, commonly found in urban parks and gardens. The surface texture of the feathers is matte and soft, with no visible gloss or iridescence.
description: A small, brown-gray bird with a conical beak, folded wings, and a straight, slightly rounded tail. Its plumage is matte and subtly graded in color.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bird's streamlined body, folded wings, and straight tail, with the beak and head profile visible.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the symmetrical tail feathers and the back of the head, with both wings tucked in.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the other profile of the bird's body and beak.
description_view_3: Frontal view displaying the beak tip, head shape, and the alignment of the body and tail.
attribute_plumage_pattern: solid with subtle gradients and slight mottling on the wings and back
